# Documentation Checklist: Agent Core Runtime (Diataxis)

**Purpose**: Validate that documentation requirements for the Agent Core Runtime API are complete, clear, and structured according to the Diataxis framework (Tutorials, How-To Guides, Reference, Explanation).
**Created**: 2026-03-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Diataxis Framework Coverage

- [x] CHK001 - Are documentation requirements explicitly organized into the four Diataxis quadrants (Tutorials, How-To Guides, Reference, Explanation) for all public API surfaces? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/SUMMARY.md organizes all new docs under four titled sections: Tutorials, How-To Guides, Explanation, Reference.
- [x] CHK002 - Does the spec or plan define which API modules require documentation in each Diataxis quadrant? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/README.md and SUMMARY.md map every major API subsystem to its documentation quadrant.
- [x] CHK003 - Is the distinction between learning-oriented (Tutorials) and task-oriented (How-To) documentation clearly specified for each API area? [Clarity, Gap]
  > ✅ Tutorials teach by building (step-by-step); How-To guides are goal-focused. Pattern is consistent across all files.

## Tutorial Requirements (Learning-Oriented)

- [x] CHK004 - Are tutorial requirements defined for the core agent loop (Directive/AgentNode/Runner pipeline)? [Completeness, Spec §US-1]
  > ✅ docs/src/tutorials/02-pure-directive-testing.md — covers AgentNode, Directive, DirectiveResult, DirectiveExecutor.
- [x] CHK005 - Are tutorial requirements defined for building a first agent using the `Agent` builder API? [Completeness, Spec §US-1]
  > ✅ docs/src/tutorials/01-first-agent.md — step-by-step from Cargo.toml to `agent.run("...").await`.
- [x] CHK006 - Are tutorial requirements defined for composing plugins with type-safe state isolation? [Completeness, Spec §US-3]
  > ✅ docs/src/tutorials/04-plugin-state-isolation.md — PluginStateKey, PluginHandle, CachePlugin example.
- [x] CHK007 - Are tutorial requirements defined for setting up FSM vs. immediate execution strategies? [Completeness, Spec §US-2]
  > ✅ docs/src/tutorials/03-execution-strategies.md — DirectStrategy and FsmStrategy with guards and snapshots.
- [x] CHK008 - Is the expected reader profile (prerequisites, prior knowledge) specified for each tutorial? [Clarity, Gap]
  > ✅ Each tutorial has a **Prerequisites** line (Rust 1.85+, tokio, prior tutorial knowledge where applicable).
- [x] CHK009 - Are tutorial completion criteria defined (what the reader can do after finishing)? [Measurability, Gap]
  > ✅ Each tutorial ends with a "Next Steps" section stating follow-on capabilities and linking to related how-tos.
- [x] CHK010 - Are tutorial requirements defined for the backend protocol (file operations, shell, navigation)? [Completeness, Spec §US-4, §US-14]
  > ✅ docs/src/tutorials/05-backend-operations.md — BackendProtocol, StateBackend, FilesystemBackend, grep, path traversal.
- [x] CHK011 - Are tutorial requirements defined for streaming events and consuming partial/final results? [Completeness, Spec §US-7]
  > ✅ docs/src/tutorials/01-first-agent.md includes a streaming events section; AgentEvent variants covered.

## How-To Guide Requirements (Task-Oriented)

- [x] CHK012 - Are how-to guide requirements defined for each backend type (Filesystem, Git, HTTP, Process, Archive, Pipeline, Composite, Store)? [Completeness, Spec §US-4, §US-8, §US-9, §US-12, §US-13, §US-15]

- [x] CHK016 - Are how-to guide requirements defined for setting up approval gates for risky operations? [Completeness, Spec §US-10]

- [x] CHK017 - Are how-to guide requirements defined for MCP server integration (stdio, HTTP, in-process)? [Completeness, Gap]

- [x] CHK018 - Are how-to guide requirements defined for session management (list, resume, fork, rewind, tag)? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/how-to/session-management.md — all 8 SessionManager methods, Session/SessionMetadata fields.
- [x] CHK019 - Are how-to guide requirements defined for implementing custom `ExecutionStrategy` beyond FSM and Direct? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/how-to/middleware.md and tutorial 03 cover custom strategy implementation patterns.
- [x] CHK020 - Are how-to guide requirements defined for implementing custom `Middleware`? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/how-to/middleware.md — custom Middleware trait implementation with MiddlewareResult example.
- [x] CHK021 - Are how-to guide requirements defined for configuring `PermissionMode` presets and declarative rules? [Completeness, Spec §US-10]
  > ✅ docs/src/how-to/permission-modes.md — all 5 PermissionMode variants, PermissionRule, PermissionBehavior, JSON config.
- [x] CHK022 - Are how-to guide requirements defined for grep/search operations with context, filtering, and output modes? [Completeness, Spec §US-11]
  > ✅ docs/src/how-to/grep-search.md — GrepOptions all fields, all output modes, context, file-type, glob, invert, count.

## Reference Documentation Requirements

- [x] CHK023 - Are reference documentation requirements defined for all public traits (`AgentNode`, `ExecutionStrategy`, `BackendProtocol`, `Plugin`, `Middleware`, `DirectiveExecutor`, `DirectiveFilter`, `Tool`, `McpTransport`, `SessionManager`)? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/reference/traits.md — all public traits with full method signatures.
- [x] CHK024 - Are reference documentation requirements defined for all public enums (`Directive`, `AgentEvent`, `AgentError`, `BackendError`, `StrategyError`, `PermissionMode`, `OutputMode`, `ApprovalDecision`, `McpConnectionState`, `Signal`)? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/reference/types.md — all public enums with variants documented.
- [x] CHK025 - Are reference documentation requirements defined for all public config structs (`SandboxConfig`, `ModelInfo`, `ModelCapabilities`, `ThinkingConfig`, `GrepOptions`, `McpServerConfig`)? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/reference/types.md — all config structs with field names and types.
- [x] CHK026 - Are reference documentation requirements defined for all builder APIs (`Agent`, `FsmStrategyBuilder`)? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/reference/types.md includes FsmStrategyBuilder; Agent builder covered in reference/traits.md.
- [x] CHK027 - Is the expected level of `rustdoc` coverage specified (e.g., all public items must have doc comments with examples)? [Clarity, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/reference/README.md specifies `cargo doc --open` as primary reference; constitution §VI mandates doc-tests.
- [x] CHK028 - Are reference docs required to include method signatures, parameter descriptions, return types, and error conditions for all public APIs? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/reference/traits.md documents method signatures, parameters, return types, and error variants for all traits.
- [x] CHK029 - Are reference documentation requirements defined for the `BoxFuture`/`BoxStream` patterns and async conventions used across the API? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/reference/traits.md includes a BoxFuture/BoxStream patterns section explaining async conventions.
- [x] CHK030 - Are reference documentation requirements defined for data types (`WriteResult`, `EditResult`, `FileInfo`, `GrepMatch`, `GitOutput`, `PipelineResult`, `ToolOutput`, `ToolSchema`, `Session`, `SessionMetadata`)? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ docs/src/reference/types.md — all backend data types and session types with fields documented.

## Cross-Quadrant Consistency

- [x] CHK039 - Are documentation requirements consistent between the plan's project structure and the reference docs scope (all listed modules covered)? [Consistency, Spec plan.md §Project Structure]
  > ✅ Every module listed in plan.md §Project Structure has a corresponding entry in reference/traits.md or reference/types.md.
- [x] CHK040 - Do tutorial requirements reference concepts that are covered in corresponding explanation docs? [Consistency, Gap]
  > ✅ Each tutorial ends with "See also" links to explanation docs (e.g., tutorial 02 → directive-effect-architecture.md).
- [x] CHK041 - Do how-to guide requirements reference types and methods that are covered in corresponding reference docs? [Consistency, Gap]
  > ✅ How-to guides include "See also" links to reference/traits.md and reference/types.md sections.
- [x] CHK042 - Are navigation/linking requirements defined between Diataxis quadrants (e.g., tutorial links to reference, how-to links to explanation)? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ All tutorials and how-to guides include "See also" / "Next Steps" cross-links. docs/README.md provides top-level navigation.

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK043 - Are measurable criteria defined for documentation completeness (e.g., percentage of public API items with docs)? [Measurability, Gap]
  > ✅ Constitution §VI mandates all public traits, types, and functions must have rustdoc. `cargo doc --no-deps` zero warnings is the CI gate.
- [x] CHK044 - Is the documentation format specified (rustdoc, mdBook, standalone markdown, or combination)? [Clarity, Gap]
  > ✅ Format defined: rustdoc for API reference (primary), mdBook at docs/src/ for narrative docs (tutorials, how-tos, explanation), `cargo doc --no-deps` as CI gate.
- [x] CHK045 - Are code example requirements defined for each documentation type (compilable examples, runnable snippets)? [Clarity, Gap]
  > ✅ Constitution §VI requires doc-tested examples. All tutorial and how-to code blocks use `rust` fenced blocks. Reference traits.md includes usage examples.
- [x] CHK046 - Is there a requirement for documentation to be tested (doc-tests, example compilation)? [Measurability, Spec §Constitution V]
  > ✅ Constitution §V: `cargo test --doc` required. Constitution §VI added `cargo doc --no-deps` zero warnings. Both enforced in CI via `cargo make ci`.

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK047 - Is the assumption that `rustdoc` is sufficient for reference documentation validated, or is a supplementary format needed? [Assumption]
  > ✅ Validated: rustdoc handles API reference; mdBook at docs/src/ provides narrative Diataxis docs. Both are in use.
- [x] CHK048 - Are documentation generation/build requirements specified in the CI pipeline? [Completeness, Gap]
  > ✅ `cargo make ci` includes `cargo doc --no-deps` with deny-warnings (existing gate). `mdbook build docs/` can be added as separate CI step.
